name: skills-factory
description: Meta-skill for creating production-ready Claude Code skills using evaluation-driven development, progressive disclosure patterns, comprehensive validation, and two-Claude iterative methodology
capabilities: Initialize new skill directories with YAML templates
Validate skill structure with quick or comprehensive checks
Package skills into zip files for distribution
Install skills to personal or project locations
author: DaronVee
securitySummary: This is a legitimate meta-skill for creating Claude Code skills. All 911 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: documentation code examples (backticks for markdown formatting), filesystem path references in guides, and utility script operations. No network access, no credential handling, no obfuscation. Python scripts perform standard file operations (create, copy, zip) to user-specified directories only.
valueStatement: Building Claude Code skills from scratch requires understanding YAML structure, progressive disclosure, validation patterns, and deployment strategies. Skills Factory provides templates, validation scripts, and comprehensive guides so you can create effective skills faster.
limitations: Does not execute arbitrary code or make network requests
Requires manual trigger based on description keywords
Skills do not sync across Claude surfaces automatically
Does not generate skill content automatically

useCases.title: Create New Skills
useCases.description: Initialize skill directories with proper YAML frontmatter, progressive disclosure structure, and validation scripts

useCases.title: Standardize Team Skills
useCases.description: Establish consistent skill patterns across your team with validation rules and deployment workflows

useCases.title: Package and Deploy Skills
useCases.description: Package skills for distribution across Claude Code, Claude.ai, Claude Desktop, and Claude API

promptTemplates.title: Create New Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new skill named api-helper using Skills Factory. Initialize it in my personal skills directory.

promptTemplates.title: Validate Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate my skill at ~/.claude/skills/pdf-processor using comprehensive validation. Show me any errors.

promptTemplates.title: Package Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Package my-data-skill for distribution. Install it to personal skills and create a zip file for Claude.ai upload.

promptTemplates.title: Apply Best Practices
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my skill at ./my-skill and suggest improvements based on Skills Factory best practices.

bestPractices: Start with evaluation-driven development: define test scenarios before building your skill
Apply progressive disclosure: keep SKILL.md under 200 lines, move details to references/
Use validation scripts at every checkpoint to catch errors early
antiPatterns: Don't skip validation: always run comprehensive_validate.py before deploying
Don't over-complicate: simple skills with clear triggers work better than complex ones
Don't forget trigger terms: include specific action verbs and use cases in your description

faq.question: What surfaces support skills?
faq.answer: Claude Code, Claude.ai, Claude Desktop, and Claude API each require separate skill deployment.

faq.question: How big can a skill be?
faq.answer: Keep SKILL.md under 200 lines for optimal triggering. Reference files can be loaded when needed.

faq.question: How do I share skills with my team?
faq.answer: Commit skills to .claude/skills/ in git repo. Share zip packages for Claude.ai/Desktop.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Skills Factory only creates and validates skill files. Makes no network calls or credential access.

faq.question: Why is my skill not triggering?
faq.answer: Check that YAML frontmatter is valid and description includes specific trigger terms.

faq.question: How is this different from skill-creator?
faq.answer: Skills Factory focuses on production quality with validation, two-Claude iteration, and deployment.
